abstract: This research evaluates the quality of the e-learning system at Universitas Mahakarya Asia by applying the McCall Method to assess the platform's overall performance. Focusing on the e-learning.unmaha.ac.id portal, the study examines six critical software quality dimensions: correctness, reliability, efficiency, integrity, usability, and maintainability. The evaluation yielded a cumulative quality score of 79.6%. While the results demonstrate high levels of reliability and data integrity, they also identify specific technical gaps, particularly concerning the accuracy of information and the complexity of system maintenance. Based on these findings, we propose a series of targeted improvements to refine content correctness, streamline the user interface, and simplify backend maintenance processes. By prioritizing these enhancements, the university can ensure the platform's long-term sustainability and provide a more responsive, high-quality digital learning environment for its students.
